Title: Transcriptional Reprogramming Deploys a Compartmentalized ‘Timebomb’ in Catharanthus roseus to Fend Off Chewing Herbivores

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chemical defence against chewing herbivores; metabolic prioritization toward cold tolerance when suppressed
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; vindoline accumulates in laticifer and idioblast cells
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression/phylogeny; overexpression under low temperature; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation; protoplast transactivation assay; phospho-mutant analysis of HIVE-linked outputs
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low temperature (4°C); herbivory by Manduca sexta
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune stress allocation between alkaloid defence and cold tolerance; suppression may help preserve vindoline under cold, but may reduce cold acclimation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of HIVE specificity by phosphorylation; induced by low temperature (~50% after 24 h); represses CrMYBR2 and vindoline pathway genes under cold while promoting CrCBF1 expression through HIVE
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in leaves after 24 h low temperature
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrSnRK2.7 physically interacts with HIVE. 2. Under low temperature, CrSnRK2.7 represses CrMYBR2 and vindoline pathway genes without changing HIVE transcript abundance. 3. CrSnRK2.7 enhances HIVE transcriptional activity and shifts output toward CrCBF1/cold tolerance signaling.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S (constitutive) for overexpression constructs; GAL4-promoter reporter in protoplast assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cold-response signaling and suppresses vindoline biosynthesis under low temperature
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activation is associated with reduced vindoline pathway expression, potentially weakening herbivore chemical defence during cold

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vindoline contributes to anti-herbivore TIA 'timebomb'; CrCBF1 itself mediates cold tolerance rather than alkaloid formation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR under low temperature and HIVE/CrSnRK2.7 perturbation; promoter transactivation assay; overexpression with REL and MDA assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving cold tolerance in C. roseus or related crops, though activation may coincide with reduced investment in vindoline defence
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core gene because it is an experimentally central downstream node in the stress-reprogramming circuit, although not a direct vindoline biosynthetic gene
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by low temperature; further induced by HIVE and CrSnRK2.7 under cold
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrCBF1, but not CrCBF2/3, is cold inducible. 2. HIVE activates the CrCBF1 promoter specifically at low temperature through an E-box-like motif. 3. CrCBF1 overexpression improves cold tolerance as shown by reduced electrolyte leakage and MDA.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S (constitutive) for overexpression; native CrCBF1 promoter in reporter assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances cold tolerance, representing the alternative output of HIVE under low temperature
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Resource allocation toward cold tolerance may coincide with lower vindoline accumulation

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of insecticidal anhydrovinblastine/vinblastine-type defence after dimerization with catharanthine
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Vindoline biosynthesis within terpenoid indole alkaloid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates tabersonine at the C16 position in the vindoline pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms upstream intermediate required for vindoline biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR under low temperature/herbivory; HIVE/CrMYBR2/BREVE overexpression and VIGS; promoter-GUS transactivation assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low temperature suppresses expression; herbivory induces expression in undamaged paired leaves at normal temperature
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated by low temperature; induced in undamaged paired leaves after herbivory at normal temperature
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T16H2 is one of the vindoline pathway genes suppressed by low temperature. 2. Its expression is induced by herbivory at normal temperature, especially in undamaged paired leaves. 3. It is activated by HIVE and CrMYBR2 but repressed by BREVE.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter reporter; CaMV35S for TF effectors
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppressed during cold stress, reducing chemical defence output
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains CR-box motifs but HIVE regulation appears indirect via CrMYBR2
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway target for vindoline enhancement, especially with upstream regulatory stacking

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of insecticidal dimeric TIA defence
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Vindoline biosynthesis within terpenoid indole alkaloid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms intermediate required for vindoline biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low temperature suppresses expression; herbivory induces expression in undamaged paired leaves at normal temperature
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated by low temperature; induced by herbivory at normal temperature in UPL
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T3O expression tracks vindoline accumulation across herbivory and cold treatments. 2. HIVE and CrMYBR2 increase its transcription, whereas BREVE represses it. 3. Low temperature overrides herbivory induction and suppresses expression.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter reporter; CaMV35S for TF effectors
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Cold-sensitive expression may limit defence under low temperature
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of four focal vindoline genes used for promoter regulation assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for pathway stacking with HIVE/CrMYBR2 to boost defence alkaloids

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Component of anti-herbivore TIA timebomb via vindoline production
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Vindoline biosynthesis within terpenoid indole alkaloid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate late precursor to vindoline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; promoter truncation/mutagenesis; HIVE/CrMYBR2/BREVE transactivation; yeast one-hybrid for CrMYBR2; overexpression/VIGS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Herbivory induces expression at normal temperature; low temperature suppresses expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in UPL after herbivory; repressed by low temperature
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D4H is a central transcriptional target used to dissect the HIVE-CrMYBR2-BREVE circuit. 2. HIVE activates D4H indirectly through a CR-box-dependent CrMYBR2 mechanism. 3. BREVE directly represses D4H through an E-box, and HIVE antagonizes this repression by heterodimerization with BREVE.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native D4H promoter and mutated/truncated derivatives; CaMV35S for TF effectors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strong positive contributor to vindoline accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Cold-dependent repression diminishes defence alkaloid output
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains two E-boxes and a CR-box; especially informative for distinguishing indirect HIVE activation versus direct BREVE repression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent engineering target/promoter module for vindoline pathway activation or cold-resilient redesign
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key transcriptional control point supported, but enzyme rate-limiting status not established

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Direct producer of vindoline, a monomer of the toxic anti-herbivore dimeric TIA system
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Vindoline biosynthesis within terpenoid indole alkaloid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; HIVE/CrMYBR2/BREVE overexpression and VIGS; promoter-GUS transactivation assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low temperature suppresses expression; herbivory induces expression in UPL at normal temperature
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated by low temperature; induced by herbivory at normal temperature in UPL
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DAT expression responds positively to herbivory and negatively to low temperature, mirroring vindoline accumulation. 2. HIVE and CrMYBR2 activate DAT, while BREVE represses DAT. 3. As the terminal vindoline enzyme, DAT is part of the HIVE-centered stress-responsive output module.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter reporter; CaMV35S for TF effectors
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced expression under cold may lower herbivore defence capacity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Terminal-step enzyme, making it closely connected to end-product accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for metabolic engineering of vindoline end-product formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant at pathway end; explicit rate-limiting status not established

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vindoline is a precursor of the highly toxic vindoline-catharanthine dimer/anhydrovinblastine defense against chewing herbivores
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; vindoline accumulates in laticifer and idioblast cells
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in undamaged paired leaves after Manduca sexta herbivory; unchanged by low temperature at transcript level
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in UPL after herbivory; not induced by LT transcriptically
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Indirect transcriptional activation of vindoline genes via CrMYBR2; antagonism of BREVE repression by heterodimerization; phosphorylation-dependent switch in target specificity; proteasomal degradation under cold
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value master regulator for engineering inducible vindoline/TIA defence or tuning tradeoff between herbivory resistance and cold tolerance; phosphosite editing may uncouple cold suppression from alkaloid production
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Herbivory by Manduca sexta; low temperature; CrSnRK2.7-mediated phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CrMYBR2; CrCBF1; indirectly T16H2; T3O; D4H; DAT
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CrMYBR2 at normal temperature; indirectly activates T16H2/T3O/D4H/DAT; at low temperature phosphorylated HIVE represses CrMYBR2 and activates CrCBF1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Vindoline biosynthesis and stress-dependent transcriptional reprogramming between TIA defence and cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes vindoline accumulation at normal temperature and herbivory, but switches under cold to suppress vindoline biosynthesis and promote cold tolerance signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression; VIGS; promoter-GUS assays; promoter truncation/mutagenesis; Y2H; luciferase complementation; phospho-mutant analysis; Phos-tag phosphorylation assay; protein degradation assay; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced HIVE may increase defence alkaloid production at normal temperature but phospho-switching/degradation under cold redirects resources; manipulating HIVE may affect cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms homodimer; forms heterodimer with BREVE; interacts with CrSnRK2.7 kinase
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S (constitutive) for overexpression; GAL4-based effector in protoplast assay
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedforward-like activation of vindoline pathway through CrMYBR2 plus simultaneous sequestration of BREVE creates coordinated positive output; cold-triggered switch breaks this module
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master stress-responsive transcriptional hub that determines whether metabolic resources are allocated to vindoline/TIA defence or to cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HIVE is a herbivory-induced Subgroup IIIb bHLH TF that activates vindoline biosynthesis and increases vindoline accumulation. 2. HIVE activates CrMYBR2, which then activates vindoline genes, while HIVE also antagonizes BREVE repression through heterodimerization. 3. Under low temperature, CrSnRK2.7-mediated phosphorylation changes HIVE specificity so that it represses CrMYBR2, activates CrCBF1, and is later degraded via the 26S proteasome.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct activation of D4H by HIVE was ruled out by E-box mutation; HIVE acts indirectly on vindoline gene promoters via CrMYBR2, but directly on CrMYBR2 and CrCBF1 promoter modules
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal temperature (~25°C); low temperature (4°C); Manduca sexta herbivory
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome co-expression analysis using C. roseus data, accession SRA030483

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vindoline is a monomer precursor of insecticidal dimeric TIA defence
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; vindoline in laticifer and idioblast cells
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in undamaged paired leaves after herbivory; downregulated rapidly by low temperature
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in UPL after herbivory; downregulated after 2 h LT
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of vindoline pathway genes through CR-box-containing promoters
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target to elevate vindoline accumulation; could be stacked with HIVE or engineered for cold-insensitive expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: HIVE activation at normal temperature; low temperature suppresses expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates vindoline pathway genes, including D4H via CR-box-dependent promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes vindoline accumulation by activating late and mid vindoline pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; VIGS; promoter transactivation; promoter mutagenesis; yeast one-hybrid; RT-qPCR; LC-MS/MS
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Cold-sensitive expression limits vindoline production under low temperature
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S for overexpression; native D4H promoter in reporter/Y1H assays
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts downstream of HIVE as part of a positive transcriptional cascade toward vindoline biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct intermediate activator required for HIVE-dependent vindoline gene induction and vindoline accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrMYBR2, but not CrMYBR1, is activated by HIVE. 2. CrMYBR2 overexpression increases T16H2/T3O/D4H/DAT expression and vindoline content, whereas VIGS reduces both. 3. CrMYBR2 directly binds and activates the D4H promoter through the CR-box.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CrMYBR2 remains activatable when overexpressed under low temperature, indicating that cold regulation mainly acts upstream at the HIVE level
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Herbivory at NT; low temperature at 4°C
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-expression analysis from transcriptome resource SRA030483

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vindoline contributes to anti-herbivore dimeric TIA chemical defence
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; vindoline in laticifer and idioblast cells
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript unchanged by herbivory and low temperature
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Unaffected in damaged and paired leaves after herbivory; unaffected by low temperature at transcript level
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional repression of vindoline pathway promoters through E-box binding; repression antagonized by HIVE heterodimerization
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Repressor knockout/downregulation could elevate vindoline levels; caution that cold-induced release of repression is part of stress-allocation logic
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Basal expression; repression relieved by HIVE sequestration; indirectly released when HIVE degrades in cold
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses vindoline pathway genes; directly represses D4H through E-box 1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces vindoline pathway gene expression and vindoline accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression; VIGS; promoter-GUS assays; promoter E-box mutagenesis; Y2H; luciferase complementation; RT-qPCR; LC-MS/MS
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppressing BREVE may increase defence alkaloids but could weaken the plant's ability to downshift alkaloid investment during cold
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms homodimer; forms heterodimer with HIVE
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S for overexpression; native vindoline-gene promoters in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Functions as a constitutive brake on vindoline biosynthesis that is neutralized by HIVE under favourable defence conditions
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major direct repressor whose sequestration or release strongly determines vindoline gene expression status
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BREVE overexpression represses T16H2/T3O/D4H/DAT and lowers vindoline, while VIGS has the opposite effect. 2. BREVE directly represses the D4H promoter via an E-box motif. 3. HIVE antagonizes BREVE repression by heterodimerization; degradation of HIVE in cold likely releases BREVE to enforce suppression.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike HIVE, BREVE is not degraded under cold in the LUC fusion assay
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-expression analysis from transcriptome resource SRA030483
